sections.title: Market Pulse: Geopolitical Tensions & Fed Expectations Drive Caution

items.headline: Futures Signal Cautious Open Amid Geopolitical Concerns and Firm Fed Stance
items.summary: U.S. equity futures are trending lower this morning as investors continue to digest strong jobs data from March, reinforcing expectations that the Federal Reserve will maintain its current interest rate policy. Elevated geopolitical tensions in the Middle East, pushing oil prices higher, further contribute to a cautious market sentiment, with some analysts noting a return of 'stagflationary fears'. Not financial advice.

items.statContext: U.S. stock futures indicate a lower open for the S&P 500.
Nasdaq futures are also down, reflecting broader pre-market weakness in tech.
Dow Jones Industrial Average futures are modestly lower ahead of market open.

items.headline: Turbo Energy Surges on Continued Oil Price Rally
items.summary: Energy-related stocks are seeing continued positive momentum in pre-market trading, fueled by the ongoing surge in global crude oil prices amid Middle East tensions.

items.headline: Elektros Highlights EV Charging Tech Amid Rising Gas Prices
items.summary: Elektros ($ELEK) is gaining traction after highlighting its patented charging technology, positioning itself to address growing consumer demand for electric vehicles amidst rising gasoline prices.

items.headline: Lucid Group Declines on Q1 Delivery Disruptions
items.summary: Lucid Group ($LCID) shares are lower in pre-market after the company reported Q1 2026 production and deliveries, revealing disruptions due to a supplier quality issue with second-row seats, despite reaffirming full-year guidance.

items.headline: Ridgetech Reacts to Upcoming Reverse Stock Split
items.summary: Ridgetech ($RDGT) shares are down in pre-market following the announcement of a 1-for-150 reverse share split set to become effective on April 7, 2026.

items.headline: Fed Poised to Hold Rates, Markets Price Out 2026 Cuts
items.summary: The Federal Reserve is widely expected to hold interest rates steady at its upcoming April 29 FOMC meeting, with markets now pricing out nearly all rate cuts for 2026. This stance is influenced by a stable labor market, persistent inflation, and heightened geopolitical risks driving oil prices higher.

items.headline: March Jobs Report Strengthens Hawkish Fed Outlook
items.summary: Recently released U.S. jobs data for March showed a stronger-than-expected addition of 178,000 new jobs and a dip in the unemployment rate to 4.3%. This robust employment picture has further solidified the market's belief that the Fed will maintain higher rates for longer.

items.headline: Energy Sector Continues Outperformance Amid Geopolitical Turmoil
items.summary: Energy and utility stocks are extending their run as definitive safe havens, with the Energy Select Sector SPDR Fund (XLE) having posted a strong return in Q1 2026. Rising crude prices, exacerbated by geopolitical tensions and a 'deliverability gap' in the Strait of Hormuz, are driving this sector's strength.

items.headline: Materials Sector 'More Favored' on Manufacturing Recovery and AI Demand
items.summary: The materials sector is viewed as 'More Favored' for its potential to benefit from a recovery in global manufacturing and increased demand for specialty materials driven by data center expansion.
